title: urceolaria capitata
intro: Urceolaria capitata, also known as Utricularia capitata, is a type of carnivorous plant that belongs to the bladderwort family. It is native to Australia and is characterized by its small, yellowish-green flowers and bladder-like traps that capture small insects for nutrition. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties, including antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. The herbal preparation of Urceolaria capitata typically involves drying and infusing the plant's leaves and stems in water to create a tea or tincture that can be used to promote wound healing and reduce inflammation.
